free_text: Excitation of deep lying hole states in 11C,15O, 27Si,
and 57Ni with the (3He,a) reaction at 216 MeV
Institut de Physique Nucleaire
2.3 mm Si for dE, 8 mm Ge(Li) for E
The energy resolution was limited to 500-800 KeV,
due to the energy definition of the incident beam,
target thickness, and kinematical effects.
It was assumed that the angular distribution of
background continuum lying under the broad peak of
interest was similar to that observed near 50 MeV
excitation energy.
RIKEN Nuclear Data Group
On. Changed to lower case and 4-digit year.
EXP in SF9 deleted. FACILITY corrected.
Part of sentence in ERR-ANALYS moved to
COMMENT.
On. STATUS moved. INC-SPECT added.
The error bars do not include the
systematic errors such as arising from the
treatment of the backgroudnd continuum.
The error bars of the figures indicated
were read by a compiler.

The angular distribution of the broad bump resting
on a smooth continuum is measured. It is suggested
that the bump arises from neutron pickup in the
deeper shell 1s for 12C.
